name: Chocolate Diamonds from Death by Chocolate
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly coat the bottom and sides of 2 10x15" baking sheets with melted butter. Line each sheet with parchment paper and lightly coat with more melted butter. Set aside.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F.
3. In a double boiler over medium heat, melt remaining butter and semisweet chocolate. Cover with plastic wrap and let sit for 10-12 minutes. Remove from heat, uncover, and stir until smooth. Keep at room temperature.
4. Beat egg yolks and 1 cup sugar in an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment on high until lemon-colored and slightly thickened (about 4 minutes). Scrape sides of bowl and beat for another 2 minutes.
5. In a dry bowl, beat egg whites with whip attachment until stiff but not dry.
6. Using a rubber spatula, fold chocolate mixture into egg yolks. Add 1/4 of the egg white mixture to chocolate and gently fold in remaining egg whites until no white streaks remain.
7. Divide batter between prepared pans and smooth tops. Bake on top and middle racks for 20-22 minutes, rotating sheets halfway through. Remove from oven and let cool for 30 minutes.
8. Toast hazelnuts in a 350°F oven for 10-15 minutes, then rub with kitchen towel to remove shells. Chop nuts into 1/8" pieces.
9. Invert one cake layer onto a large cutting board and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Keep remaining cake at room temperature until needed.
10. To prepare ganache, heat cream, butter, and sugar in a saucepan over medium heat until sugar dissolves. Bring to boil, then pour boiling mixture over chocolate in bowl. Let stand 6-7 minutes until smooth. Stir in chopped hazelnuts. Keep at room temperature for filling and refrigerate reserved portion.
11. Puree raspberries and sugar in food processor for 12-15 seconds, strain through fine mesh sieve into a bowl. Cover and refrigerate until needed.
1
